title: Coral Reef Survey: Pre-Positioning Sample Kits across a Dive Supply Tree
strategies: order-to-mean baseline: stock each site to its historical mean specimen count with no vessel reserve (reproduces the evaluator's weak reference, ~50% service, always within budget)

single-echelon newsvendor: set each site to its own critical fractile q_i = mean_i + Phi^{-1}(p_i/(p_i+h_i))*std_i, cutting shortage sharply but ignoring the service target, risk pooling, and the budget headroom

two-echelon / risk-pooled safety stock: trim local stock toward the service-level floor and hold a shared vessel reserve q0 that is ferried to whichever site runs short, cutting total holding for the same protection

sample-average approximation + local search: draw own scenarios from the declared distributions and coordinate-descend q and q0 against the exact composite objective, generalizing to the held-out survey days
